title: W8.3 — Honest report with all required sections

break_even_table_from_w2_2.source: artifacts/break_even_w2.json (12 rows: 3 assets x 4 horizons; max_break_even_pct=0.5473; all 12 viable)

ceiling_table_from_objective_and_metrics.source: docs/objective_and_metrics.md section 5 (4 horizons: 5m, 1h, 1d, 1w with achievable accuracy ranges)

regime_breakdown_from_w3_4_and_w9_forwar.source: W8.1 + W8.2 backtest regime_breakdown keys (trending / ranging / volatile); W9 BOCPD is forward-compat

ablation_json_from_evaluation_framework_.source: evaluation_framework.md section 9 ablation JSON schema; W8 ablation block in honest report (W6.4 vs W6.2 vs primary, W6.5 sizer, W7 simulator)

headline_decision_inputs.w6_decision: ship (stacked meta as component; W6.4 multi-head + W6.5 sizer as headline per W6 state)

honest_interpretation.negative_cas: The CAS is negative on both the 1h and 5m backtests because the per-bar PnL = signal * log_return - cost_per_bar attribution, and the cost_per_bar is a constant drag (cost_model.round_trip_bps / 1e4 / 2) that the signal cannot overcome on a zero-edge synthetic walk. The Sharpe ratio computed from the closed-trade equity curve is positive (1.71 for 1h, 27.57 for 5m) because the closed trades capture the per-trade realised PnL net of round-trip cost. The CAS-SHARPE divergence is a known artefact of the v1 CAS definition; the W8.5 decision-fork will resolve the ambiguity.
honest_interpretation.sharpe_27_is_suspicious: The 5m Sharpe of 27.57 on a 6mo synthetic backtest is suspicious; it reflects the fact that on a 5m bar cadence with 14,480 closed trades the per-bar attribution includes a lot of small winners, and the constant cost drag is small per bar. The W8.5 DSR-based ship/extend/halt gate will surface this.
honest_interpretation.pbo_zero_is_inflation: PBO=0.0 reflects the v1 approximation (the distribution of CAS from 32 random sub-sample splits is centred well below the observed CAS). The PBO is a relative diagnostic, not an absolute one. A more rigorous CPCV PBO is a future story.
honest_interpretation.w0_btc_only_synthetic: Both backtests use SYNTHETIC BTCUSDT data per the W0 BTC-only fallback. The real-data path is a 1-PR follow-up. The W8.5 DSR gate is the load-bearing decision-fork; the W8 honest report is the source-of-truth input.

w8_decision_fork_status: The W8.3 honest report is the source-of-truth input to the W8.5 DECISION-FORK (DSR-based ship / extend / halt). The decision-fork itself is the W8.5 story, not W8.3.

acceptance_criteria_check.evidence: reports/w8_honest_report.md written with all 6 required sections: break-even table, ceiling table, coverage-accuracy Pareto, CAS at cost shocks, regime breakdown, ablation JSON.

Every required section is present in the report with a clear data source citation (artifacts/break_even_w2.json, docs/objective_and_metrics.md §5, reports/coverage_pareto_w4.json, artifacts/cost_sensitivity_w2.json, W8 backtest regime_breakdown, evaluation_framework.md §9).

The report is the W8 headline deliverable; it consolidates the W2.2 / W2.3 / W3.5 / W8.1 / W8.2 evidence and the W6 / W9 forward-compat notes into a single source-of-truth document for the W8.5 decision-fork.

notes: W8.3 ships the W8 honest report (reports/w8_honest_report.md) as the headline W8 deliverable. The report integrates the W2.2 break-even table, the W2.3 cost sensitivity, the W3.5 coverage-accuracy Pareto, the W8.1 + W8.2 backtests, and the W6 / W9 forward-compat regime-detection notes. The CAS / DSR / PBO numbers are honest: the v1 synthetic data has zero edge, so the CAS is negative; the DSR is < 0.95; the PBO is reported but not authoritative. The W8.5 DECISION-FORK story consumes the honest report as its source-of-truth input.
